# Slurm test jobs

Minimal `sbatch` test jobs using `sleep` to compare baseline vs funded account/QoS behavior.

If you do not specify an account, the job should run on the default account with its default QoS; for a funded project, add `#SBATCH --account=proj_A` in the script or submit with `sbatch --account=proj_A script.sh`.

To check queue wait after submission:

```bash
sacct -j <jobid> --format=JobID,JobName,Partition,Account,QOS,Submit,Start,Elapsed,State
```

## Tiny and fast

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=tiny-2m
#SBATCH --time=00:02:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=1
#SBATCH --mem=256M

date
sleep 120
date
```

## Short small job

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=small-10m
#SBATCH --time=00:10:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=1
#SBATCH --mem=1G

date
sleep 600
date
```

## Medium job

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=medium-1h
#SBATCH --time=01:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=2
#SBATCH --mem=4G

date
sleep 3600
date
```

## Long single-node job

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=long-8h
#SBATCH --time=08:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4
#SBATCH --mem=8G

date
sleep 28800
date
```

## Large CPU job

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=large-cpu-2h
#SBATCH --time=02:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=32
#SBATCH --mem=32G

date
sleep 7200
date
```

## Large memory job

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=large-mem-2h
#SBATCH --time=02:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=8
#SBATCH --mem=128G

date
sleep 7200
date
```

## Same script, funded account test

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=funded-check-30m
#SBATCH --time=00:30:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4
#SBATCH --mem=8G
#SBATCH --account=proj_A

date
sleep 1800
date
```

## Same shape, explicit partition test

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=nodes-check-30m
#SBATCH --time=00:30:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4
#SBATCH --mem=8G
#SBATCH --partition=nodes

date
sleep 1800
date
```
